normalized_title: Operational Policy 1.1 Subdivision of Land - General Principles
scope_summary: WAPC operational policy setting out the general principles used in determining applications for the subdivision of land across Western Australia, including freehold, survey-strata, strata and leasehold lots under Part 10 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 and the Strata Titles Act 1985. It covers consistency with long-term planning goals, context and site analysis, site-responsive design, structure plans, super lot subdivisions, development contributions, lawful development, leasehold strata, utility servicing, vehicular access, development capability and implementation/review procedures.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: land holdings identified or zoned for future urban purposes (presumption against super lot subdivision where holding is generally less than 150 ha)

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum super lot size for whole-of-holding subdivision
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: subdivision of whole land holding into super lots where SPP 3.6 infrastructure arrangements are established
residential lots below this size and/or of irregular configuration may need to demonstrate a dwelling can be accommodated via development application and/or building plans
leasehold (survey-strata) and leasehold (strata) schemes under the Strata Titles Act 1985 (inclusive of any postponement)

definitions: subdivision (s1.1: includes creation of new lot(s), amalgamation of existing lots, and other dealings in land requiring WAPC approval, covering freehold, survey-strata, strata and leasehold lots)
structure plan (s3.4: framework for integrated subdivision and development of a major area)

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
State Planning Policy 1 State Planning Framework (SPP 1)
State Planning Policy 3.6 Development Contributions for Infrastructure
State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes - V1
Transfer of Land Act (s70A; text prints '1993', the Act is 1893)
IPWEA Local Government Guidelines for Subdivisional Development October 2017 (edition 2.3)

extracted text prints 'Transfer of Land Act 1993' where the statute is the Transfer of Land Act 1893 (likely source typo or OCR error)
